source: Ban Co Yeu Chinh Phu / Bo Thong Tin va Truyen Thong
note: Vietnam PQC-specific guidelines are still evolving. This file aggregates known requirements and recommendations.

organizations.name_vi: Bo Thong Tin va Truyen Thong
Trung tam Ung cuu Khan cap May tinh Viet Nam

current_requirements.name_vi: Luat An toan Thong tin Mang 2018
requirements: Government systems must use approved cryptographic algorithms
Critical information systems require periodic security assessment
Encryption products must be certified by Ban Co Yeu
Information systems classified into 5 security levels
Annual security audit required for level 4-5 systems

known_initiatives: Ban Co Yeu monitoring NIST PQC standardization
Academic research at VNUHCM, HUST on lattice-based cryptography
No official PQC migration timeline announced yet

recommendations_for_vietnam.action: Inventory all cryptographic algorithms in critical government systems
recommendations_for_vietnam.rationale: Cannot plan migration without knowing current state

recommendations_for_vietnam.action: Enable hybrid key exchange (X25519Kyber768) on public-facing TLS services
recommendations_for_vietnam.rationale: Low risk, backward compatible, protects against harvest-now-decrypt-later

recommendations_for_vietnam.action: Upgrade SSH to use sntrup761x25519 hybrid key exchange
recommendations_for_vietnam.rationale: Supported in OpenSSH 9.0+, low implementation effort

recommendations_for_vietnam.action: Plan PKI migration timeline aligned with NIST standards
recommendations_for_vietnam.rationale: Certificate migration is highest effort, needs advance planning

recommendations_for_vietnam.action: Benchmark PQC performance on local hardware
recommendations_for_vietnam.rationale: Vietnam infrastructure may have different performance characteristics than Western benchmarks

asean_context.singapore: CSA issued PQC advisory in 2023, active migration planning
asean_context.japan: CRYPTREC monitoring PQC, expected to follow NIST
asean_context.vietnam: Early awareness, this tool aims to accelerate assessment
